Title: Sabah allocates RM102m for food security to tackle cost of living
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government will focus on food security to mitigate the medium- and long-term impacts of the rising cost of living.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that under the 13th Malaysia Plan, RM102.86 million has been allocated for food security projects.
"The main focus is to increase agricultural, livestock, and fisheries production to reduce dependence on imports, which are often exposed to global price fluctuations.
"The ultimate goal is to improve the Self-Sufficiency Level (SSL) and ensure a stable food supply under any economic conditions," he said.
Hajiji said the government, through the Sabah Economic Development and Investment Authority (Sedia), has identified Keningau, Tenom, Nabawan, Tongod, and Tambunan as Food Production Valleys.
The initiative aims to optimise state land for large-scale production to stabilise the local market supply.
He was replying to a question from Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal (Warisan-Senallang) regarding immediate state actions to protect the people from the rising cost of living following higher global oil prices.
Hajiji also highlighted high-impact projects such as the Keningau Integrated Livestock Centre (KILC), which focuses on beef cattle farming, and Sabah Livestock Dairies Breeders Sdn Bhd in Sook, which focuses on dairy farming.
"These efforts are expected to reduce dependence on imported meat and dairy products while stabilising market prices," he said.
He also encouraged the public to adopt self-sustaining practices through the Kebun Dapur (Kitchen Garden) programme to reduce household expenses.
On logistics, Hajiji said collaboration with the federal government for the Sepanggar Port expansion is expected to increase cargo capacity and reduce logistics surcharges.
"Indirectly, this measure helps curb price increases caused by sea transportation costs," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DBKL to address traffic congestion at critical locations through road upgrading projects
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is implementing three targeted road projects at critical locations identified based on an analysis of current traffic flows and projected vehicle growth.
Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said all the projects focus on resolving bottleneck issues that affect the daily movement of city residents.
He said the project involves the construction of a road and tunnel from Jalan Kiara 7 to the SPRINT Highway (Penchala Link) in Segambut to address congestion on Jalan Desa Kiara and the Mont Kiara area which is at a critical level (LOS F) due to access and exit constraints as well as increased development density.
"To address this issue, DBKL will build an alternative route that connects Jalan Kiara 7 directly to the SPRINT Highway (E23 Penchala Link). This project involves the construction of a 562-metre new road as well as tunnel and bridge structures.
"Based on traffic simulations conducted, this project has the potential to improve the level of road service from LOS F to LOS C, thus reducing travel time and redistributing traffic flow in the area," he said in a statement today.
Fadlun said the project would start in the fourth quarter of this year and is expected to be completed within two years.
In addition, DBKL is also carrying out works to upgrade the Seputeh exit from the Pantai Baru Expressway (NPE) to the Kuala Lumpur-Seremban Expressway following congestion in the area, particularly involving traffic flows from Jalan Kuchai Maju, Jalan Desa and Salak which have directly affected peak hour travel.
He said the upgrading work involves adding one lane to three lanes and upgrade the road alignment and bridge along about 600 metres to increase capacity and smooth traffic flow.
He said the project is expected to start in the fourth quarter of this year and be completed within a year.
He said the project also involves upgrading the intersection of Jalan 7/24 and Jalan Lingkaran Tengah 2 (MRR2) in Wangsa Melawati which is expected to start this June and be completed within four months.
"The proposed upgrade involves widening the entrance and exit ramps, improving the intersection design and increasing road safety elements. DBKL is currently in the final stages of finalising the design before submitting it to the Selangor State Public Works Department for approval," he said.
Fadlun said the three projects reflect DBKL's new approach of putting development proceeds back to the people through infrastructure investments that solve the city's real problems. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak maintains zero tolerance on bullying after MRSM incident
Article:
PERAK: The state government will await a full report before deciding on action over an alleged bullying incident at Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) Sultan Azlan Shah in Kuala Kangsar.
State Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairudin Abu Hanipah said authorities have yet to receive a comprehensive account of the case.
"I have yet to receive a complete report regarding the alleged bullying incident said to have taken place at MRSM Sultan Azlan Shah in Kuala Kangsar, based on a statement by Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi.
"In my view, the state government and the State Education Department have zero tolerance when it comes to bullying cases.
"Whoever is at fault must face the consequences. Those responsible must accept the outcome, as we do not want a culture of bullying or gangsterism to spread among our children," he told reporters after attending the One Third High Talks and Thank You Teacher Programme at a hotel here today.
Also present was State Education Department director Zulkafli Mohamed Mokhtar.
On Saturday, Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) identified the perpetrators involved in the bullying case at the school, which went viral on social media.
Khairudin said education must go beyond academic achievement, stressing the importance of character-building in shaping responsible citizens.
"We want students to excel academically, but they must also have good manners and strong values. These are crucial in preparing them to become mature individuals who can adapt to society," he said.
He said that any student proven to be involved in bullying would face disciplinary action in accordance with school rules and ministry guidelines.
"If the allegations are substantiated, the student must accept punishment based on existing regulations and circulars issued by the school and the ministry," he said.
On the next course of action, Khairudin said authorities would first study the report before deciding on further steps.
"We will assess the findings together with the department. If there is evidence of wrongdoing, appropriate action will be taken based on established procedures," he said.
He added that the Education Ministry has a structured reporting mechanism for bullying cases, which includes timelines of one, three and seven days for reports, followed by intervention programmes.
"If those measures do not resolve the matter, further action will be taken in line with ministry guidelines.
"If you do it, you must face the consequences. There will be no compromise when it comes to bullying," he said.
The case came to light following a complaint by a Facebook user, Khairul Nizam, alleging bullying at the school, including cutting of uniforms, anonymous threats, vandalism and psychological abuse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World's first AI-powered homes to debut in i-City at competitive pricing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's residential landscape is set for a significant shift as I-Bhd prepares to launch AI Living, the country's first robot-enabled residential development, at i-City this October, with prices starting from RM1,050 per sq ft.
Positioned as a core component of a 1.75 hectare mixed-use development with a gross development value (GDV) of RM1.2 billion, AI Living, also the world's first of its kind residential project, will comprise a 46-storey tower housing 500 residential units.
The unit mix includes 164 two-bedroom apartments (650 sq ft), 170 mid-sized units (800 sq ft), and 166 larger 3+1 layouts (1,160 sq ft), targeting a broad spectrum of urban buyers.
Beyond its residential offering, the development integrates office space and a retail hub, with the commercial components slated for launch in the first half of 2027, reinforcing i-City's positioning as a self-contained urban centre.
Group executive chairman and founder Tan Sri Lim Kim Hong said AI Living represents a strategic evolution of i-City from a digital township into an AI- and robotics-enabled ecosystem.
Unlike conventional developments where smart features are retrofitted, AI Living has been conceived as an "AI-native" project from inception. This approach aligns building design, infrastructure, and long-term operations with the functional requirements of artificial intelligence and robotics.
"AI Living is designed from the outset as an AI-native residential environment, integrating architecture and systems to support intelligent technologies both now and in the future," Lim said, adding that pricing remains competitive with conventional projects despite the advanced features.
The project builds on I-Bhd's broader AI roadmap, following the launch of its AI World Experience Centre earlier this year, now equipped with nearly 100 robots. The facility serves as a public-facing hub for AI education and innovation, underscoring the group's commitment to integrating technology, talent development, and infrastructure in line with the Fourth Industrial Revolution (IR4.0).
From a property perspective, AI Living introduces a dual-layer value proposition: physical real estate combined with embedded digital infrastructure. Each unit is equipped with a "vitality system", pre-installed wiring, sensors, and automation capabilities, allowing residents to adopt AI services at their discretion.
Core home functions, including lighting, appliances, and climate control, will be automated and accessible via voice commands, mobile applications, or in-home panels, alongside AI-driven scheduling tools.
In addition, residents can opt into subscription-based robotic services, including in-home cleaning and delivery, effectively creating a new category of managed living services.
[[nid:1427289]]
To support its robotics ecosystem, I-Bhd has partnered with AgiBot, which has already supplied humanoid robots to the experience centre, while those designated for AI Living remain in the pilot phase. The AI Living robots will be programmed for domestic assistance and caregiving roles, particularly for elderly and assisted-living segments.
At the township level, AI integration extends to security and infrastructure, with features such as facial recognition access, biometric entry systems, smart surveillance, and digital visitor management. Safety systems will also be interconnected, enabling coordinated, real-time responses across fire protection, lifts, and security networks.
Lim noted that the transformation of i-City reflects broader shifts in the property sector, where digital infrastructure is no longer a differentiator but a baseline, and AI is emerging as the next value driver.
"When i-City was first developed, digital meant connectivity. Today, AI and robotics are becoming core operating layers of the economy," he said.
Importantly, I-Bhd is taking a long-term asset management approach rather than a traditional build-and-sell model, positioning AI Living not just as a residential project, but as a foundational component of a future-ready urban ecosystem.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Akmal backs BN pullout, says move rooted in Umno principles
Article:
AYER KEROH: Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has backed the move by 14 Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen to withdraw their support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, describing it as a principled decision.
Dr Akmal, who is also BN Youth chief, said the firm stand taken by Negri Sembilan Umno proves the party will defend its core struggle for religion, race, and the royal institution, even at the risk of losing power.
"I congratulate the Negri Sembilan Umno leadership for having the courage to make a decision based on party principles.
"We will not remain silent if the sovereignty of the rulers is challenged. This is a fundamental principle that must be defended under any circumstances, he said.
He was speaking at a press conference after attending the Melaka state assembly sitting at Seri Negeri here today.
Earlier today, Negri Sembilan BN chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias announced that all 14 BN assemblymen had unanimously withdrawn their support for Aminuddin, who is also the Sikamat assemblyman, following a crisis involving state customary institutions.
Dr Akmal, the Merlimau assemblyman, noted that Umno's participation in the unity government was previously driven by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's decree during a political stalemate, rather than a desire for power.
He said that while Umno initially rejected cooperation with certain parties, it joined the government for national stability.
"But that does not mean we sacrifice our principles. Umno's principles remain and will not be compromised," he said.
He stressed that being in government or the opposition was secondary to upholding the party's values.
"Even if we have to lose power, Umno will defend its principles. We can be the government or the opposition, but our principles are not for sale," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Higher Education Ministry finalising transfer of Form 6, matriculation programmes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is finalising the legal and administrative framework to bring the Form Six and matriculation programmes under its jurisdiction.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said this followed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's recent announcement on moving pre-university programmes from the Education Ministry to the Higher Education Ministry.
"Our priority is to manage the transition process, which involves legal provisions since these programmes were under the Education Ministry," Zambry said at a media briefing at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
On student welfare, he said the ministry would implement the government's decision to increase scholarship allowances for Form Six and matriculation students.
He said the transfer of the programmes to the Higher Education Ministry would not disrupt the financial aid.
"On scholarships, I do not foresee any major issues. We will adhere to the government's decision to increase scholarship assistance for them."
Zambry said once the transfer was done, students would benefit from the ministry's expertise.
"The second part of the transition is integrating matriculation and Form Six into the ministry.
"We have experts ready to support these students as they prepare for higher education."
Placing the Form Six and matriculation programmes under the Higher Education Ministry is expected to streamline students' pathways into public universities and ensure a more cohesive transition into tertiary education.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIA's passenger movements grows 14.4pct to 16.9mil  in Q1
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded strong growth across its local network in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, with Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) registering 16.9 million passenger movements, a 14.4 per cent increase compared to the same period last year.
In a statement today, MAHB said that in March alone, local airports handled 9.1 million passenger movements, of which 5.6 million travelled through KLIA, while the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period from March 14 to 29 saw more than 3.1 million passengers passing through the airport.
MAHB managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said the continued growth across the network demonstrates the increasing relevance of Malaysia's airports as regional gateways.
"Our priority is to match this momentum with the right capacity, seamless operations and targeted improvements on the ground, ensuring we remain competitive while unlocking further growth opportunities for Malaysia," he said.
MAHB said during the same month, Penang International Airport (PEN) saw an 11 per cent rise in international passenger numbers, moving from 371,601 in February to 412,958. Meanwhile, Kota Kinabalu International Airport (BKI) recorded a two per cent increase in domestic passenger numbers, rising from 541,248 in February to 551,049.
It said this contributed to a total of 27.4 million passenger movements across the local network in the first quarter.
"MAHB's wholly owned international subsidiary, Istanbul Sabiha Gokcen International Airport (ISGIA), recorded 11.7 million passengers in the first quarter of 2026, representing a 12 per cent increase compared to the corresponding period last year," it added.
Meanwhile, the airport operator said ongoing improvements to airport facilities and services are being implemented to enhance passenger comfort and operational efficiency across its network.
At KLIA, it said a large-scale ketupat-inspired installation brought festive cheer to the terminal, seamlessly blending cultural identity with modern design. Regional airports are also seeing targeted improvements, with Langkawi (LGK) and Kuching (KCH) introducing upgraded baby care rooms, while Alor Star (AOR), Kuantan (KUA), Labuan (LBU) and Lahad Datu (LDU) now welcome passengers with refreshed check-in counters.
According to MAHB, Operational enhancements have also been introduced to improve passenger flow, including the early baggage drop-off service for Malaysia Airlines passengers at Kota Kinabalu International Airport, providing greater flexibility and a smoother pre-departure experience.
Alongside these efforts, it said connectivity across the network continued to expand in March with the addition of three new international airlines, namely AeroDili from Timor-Leste, Air Algerie from Algeria and Chongqing Airlines from China.
MAHB said new routes introduced include Nanjing–Kuala Lumpur, Chongqing–Kota Kinabalu, Wuxi–Kuala Lumpur, Batam–Kuala Lumpur, Phuket–Penang, Guiyang–Kuala Lumpur, Guiyang–Kuching, Colombo–Kuala Lumpur and Yangzhou–Kuala Lumpur.
"In Penang, the introduction of the Qingdao–Penang route further strengthened the airport's direct connectivity to China while complementing existing services within the Malaysia network," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bumiputera Education Congress to review key education policies in July
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Bumiputera Education Congress 2026 will be held in July to review key education policies and strengthen the national Bumiputera education agenda.
Head Secretariat of the Bumiputera Education Congress 2026 Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi said the one-day congress will bring together more than 4,000 participants from universities, government agencies, NGOs and political parties.
He said the congress will feature 25 presenters covering five main themes, including a review of existing policies such as matriculation and fully residential schools.
"We want to assess whether these policies should be continued, strengthened or improved," he said.
He said the outcome of the congress will not remain at the discussion stage but will be translated into actionable policy recommendations through a dedicated government-level secretariat.
"This is to ensure the resolutions from the congress are implemented effectively as part of the national education agenda," he added.
Separately, he said a Leadership Summit programme will be held to discuss leadership, education and youth empowerment at 3pm today at Dewan Azman Hashim, Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) Kuala Lumpur.
It is expected to gather about 700 to 800 student leaders, including international football star Mesut Özil, alongside Najibuddin Bilal Al-Bakar, Wabi Fadlina Syed Kabrad and the Turkish Higher Education Minister.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan DAP leaders hold emergency meeting over state crisis
Article:
SEREMBAN: Several Negri Sembilan DAP assemblymen were seen gathering at the state DAP headquarters in Kemayan Square here today following Barisan Nasional's (BN) withdrawal of support for the state government.
The meeting is believed to be an emergency session to discuss the leadership crisis involving Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, who is also Chennah assemblyman, arrived around 2pm to chair the meeting.
Loke briefly told reporters that he would meet Aminuddin at the Menteri Besar's official residence immediately after the session.
A press conference is expected to be held following the meeting with Aminuddin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Edotco Malaysia's unit Vista Bumiria leads telecom infra drive in Terengganu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Edotco Malaysia's subsidiary Vista Bumiria Sdn Bhd has been approinted as state-backed company to drive the development and expansion of telecommunications infrastructure in Terengganu.
The Terengganu state government also designated Alam Mindscape Mobile Sdn Bhd as the one stop agency for telecommunications towers.
Vista Bumiria will lead the deployment of fibre networks and towers, serving as a central platform for mobile network operators (MNOs) to expand coverage across both urban and rural areas.
Alam Mindscape will streamline approvals and coordination processes, reducing deployment bottlenecks and enhancing execution efficiency.
"This strategic move reflects Terengganu's commitment to accelerating the rollout of high-quality, future-ready digital infrastructure, while ensuring more coordinated, efficient and sustainable development across the state," Edotco said in a statement.
Further strengthening governance, Tengku Datuk Seri Muhammad Mua'az ibni Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin has been appointed as Vista Bumiria chairman, reinforcing leadership alignment in advancing the state's digital infrastructure agenda.
Edotco group chief executive officer Adlan Tajudin said the structure introduces a more effective model for infrastructure rollout at the state level.
"As a regional digital infrastructure player, Edotco is committed to bringing our expertise to support a more efficient and coordinated rollout of telecommunications infrastructure.
"Ultimately, this is about delivering better connectivity for the people and supporting Terengganu's long-term digital growth," he said.
Adlan added that the model positions Terengganu to become a leading example of state-driven digital infrastructure development in Malaysia, enabling a more coordinated and efficient approach to connectivity rollout.
Edotco will work closely with its MNO partners to accelerate deployment across the state, ensuring that connectivity in Terengganu is reliable, high-quality and accessible to all communities.
The collaboration marks Edotco Malaysia's first formalised platform to build and expand telecommunications infrastructure in Terengganu.
This lays the foundation for scalable, 5G-ready networks that will support the state's next phase of economic growth and digital advancement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Final verdict for Syed Saddiq appeal on June 30
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Federal Court has fixed June 30 to deliver its final verdict in the appeal involving Muar member of Parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin confirmed the date when contacted.
The appeal before the apex court stems from the prosecution's bid to overturn the Court of Appeal's decision which acquitted Syed Saddiq of charges linked to criminal breach of trust, misappropriation of property and money laundering involving Armada funds.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uzma Engineering secures Petronas Carigali contract for Bayan Phase 3 PWIM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Uzma Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Uzma Engineering Sdn Bhd, has secured a Portable Water Injection Module (PWIM) contract from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd under the Bayan Redevelopment Phase 3 project, strengthening its production solutions segment.
The contract covers the provision of leasing, as well as operation and maintenance services for the PWIM.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the energy and technology company said the scope includes the design, engineering, procurement, construction and transportation of one PWIM unit within a one-year period, followed by installation, commissioning, as well as operation and maintenance.
It said the contract is for a duration of seven years, with an option for a further two-year extension.
The contract is effective from March 27, 2026, and will remain valid for up to eight years unless extended or earlier terminated in accordance with its terms and conditions.
Uzma group chief executive officer Datuk Kamarul Redzuan Muhamed said the contract reflects the group's continued efforts to strengthen its capabilities across the production solutions space, positioning it to support operators at various stages of field development.
"As part of our second Five-Year Plan, our focus remains on strengthening our core businesses, expanding our capabilities, and building a more resilient portfolio anchored on long cycle, recurring income assets," he said.
Following the award, Uzma will continue to deploy its portable and modular water injection solutions to support efficient field development.
The PWIM solution provides flexibility in deployment while maintaining operational reliability, particularly in mature and redeveloping fields where optimisation is critical.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shafee tight-lipped after Najib drops appeal in addendum case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah remained tight-lipped following Datuk Seri Najib Razak's decision to withdraw his appeal over the failed bid to serve the remainder of his prison sentence under house arrest.
The former prime minister's lead counsel declined to comment on the development when met at the Palace of Justice here today.
"There are things that are beyond me. I cannot comment. I do not know," said the senior lawyer.
Earlier, it was reported that the
former prime minister had withdrawn his appeal
against the High Court's dismissal of his bid to serve the remainder of his jail term under house arrest.
Law firm Messrs Shafee & Co, which represented Najib, said in a letter to the Court of Appeal that the former prime minister intended to withdraw the appeal without leave to refile and without any order as to costs.
No reason was given for the withdrawal.
When pressed further, Shafee again declined to elaborate.
"You have quoted all that before. Why this one you need special answer. You all want to make it more interesting. I cannot comment," he said.
Najib is currently serving his sentence at Kajang Prison after being convicted of misappropriating RM42 million belonging to SRC International Sdn Bhd.
On Dec 22 last year,
High Court judge Alice Loke dismissed Najib's judicial review application
to compel the government and six others to implement an addendum order allowing him to serve the remainder of his sentence under house arrest.
Loke said the King cannot exercise the royal prerogative of mercy independently and must act on the advice of the Pardons Board, in accordance with Article 42 of the Federal Constitution.
She said Article 42 prescribes a strict, constitutionally mandated decision-making process for clemency.
She said the Pardons Board must convene, consider the written opinion of the Attorney-General, and formally tender its advice before the King may make any decision.
She said the addendum order was neither deliberated nor decided at the 61st meeting of the Pardons Board, which had considered only Najib's application for a full pardon and a reduction of his prison term.
She noted that the minutes of the meeting confirmed that the only decision made by the King was a reduction of Najib's imprisonment term and fine.
The addendum order was said to have been consented to by the 16th Yang di-Pertuan Agong, which would have allowed Najib to serve the remainder of his sentence under house arrest.
In the judicial review, Najib sought a mandamus order to compel the government to enforce the addendum by transferring him from Kajang Prison to his residence to continue serving his sentence under house arrest.
Among the reliefs sought was an order for respondents to provide a copy of the royal order issued by the former King on the reduction of his sentence and fine.
Najib, who has been serving his sentence at Kajang Prison since Aug 23, 2022, filed a petition for a royal pardon on Sept 2, 2022.
The Pardons Board subsequently reduced his prison sentence from 12 years to six years, while his fine was lowered from RM210 million to RM50 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese citizen held for driving against traffic in Sungai Petani
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A Chinese citizen believed to be under the influence of alcohol was detained after driving against the flow of traffic and crashing into another vehicle at Jalan Lencongan Barat last night.
The incident went viral on social media today after the man's dangerous driving was captured on camera.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police received a report on the two-car collision at 11.30pm.
Initial investigations found that the suspect, a company director in his 50s, had driven a black Perodua Myvi against traffic for about 500 metres.
"As a result, the man's car collided head-on with another vehicle at the scene. The driver is suspected of being under the influence of alcohol, and police are currently tracing his next of kin," he said in a statement today.
Hanyan said the case is being investigated under Section 42 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving, and Section 45C(6) of the same Act for refusing to provide breath or blood samples.
The suspect sustained minor facial injuries and has been admitted to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital.
The driver of the other car escaped uninjured, Hanyan said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gold Li eyes RM15mil from IPO, sets price at 13 sen a share
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Gold Li Holdings Bhd is set to raise up to RM15.21 million from its debut on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia scheduled for May 18.
The company fixed its initial public offering (IPO) price at 13 sen per share, according to its prospectus launched today.
The IPO will entail 153 million shares, comprising 117 million new shares and 36 million existing shares for Bumiputera investors approved by Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry.
Of the 117 million new shares, 30 million will be available for the Malaysian public, six million for the property company's eligible persons, 39 million for Bumiputera investors approved by the ministry, and the remaining 42 million for the private placement to selected investors.
According to the prospectus, RM11.21 million of the raised proceeds, equivalent to 73.7 per cent of the total amount, will be utilised as working capital for property development.
This includes three ongoing projects at Taman Permatang Pasir II, Taman Kesang Mawar and Taman Naib Kadir Suria as well as one future project located at Mukim Linau.
The remaining 26.3 per cent, or RM4 million, will be allocated for the reimbursement of listing expenses.
The milestone marks an important step forward as the company adapts to changing market demand while continuing to focus on landed properties.
With 39.6 hectares of future project and 23.9 hectares of landbank, Gold Li said it remains well-positioned to support its long-term growth and sustain a steady pipeline of developments in their company's core markets.
Upon full subscription, the Johor-focused property developer's share capital would be valued at RM122.96 million placing its total market capitalisation at RM78 million.
Applications for the retail tranche will close on May 5.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media Council resolves five public complaints since establishment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has received seven public complaints since its establishment in June last year, ranging from reporting accuracy to the unauthorised use of images.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said five of the complaints have been resolved while two remain under review.
She said all cases are now channelled through the MMC's official platform to ensure systematic recording and assessment.
"To further enhance transparency and accountability, all complaints are submitted through the official MMC platform, ensuring each case is handled in a structured and professional manner," she said at the opening of the 21st Confederation of Asean Journalists here today.
Teo said the council, as an independent self-regulatory body, aims to reinforce ethical standards and safeguard media freedom.
She said the MMC should continue to serve as the main platform and first point of reference for handling public complaints involving the media.
However, she added that while complaints against the media would be handled by the council, criminal investigations and prosecutions remain under the jurisdiction of the police and the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC).
She said the police retain the authority to investigate complaints and refer cases to the A-GC for further action, including prosecution where necessary.
Teo expressed hope that as the MMC grows stronger, it could become the first layer in managing issues involving the media.
"I hope the Media Council can continue to play its role and become the main platform, or first platform, to manage and review complaints from the public," she said.
She also stressed the importance of mutual respect between enforcement agencies and media practitioners to ensure journalists can carry out their duties professionally and without unnecessary interference.
Teo cited a recent incident in Cheras involving journalists at an entertainment outlet, where police officers had allegedly asked reporters to delete videos and photographs.
"That should not have happened, but I am relieved that the police apologised to members of the media.
"This is what we want — mutual respect, allowing the media to carry out their work while recognising that there can be instances of less ethical reporting."
Teo added that complaints did not necessarily come from the government, but often from the public, making the MMC an important avenue for addressing such concerns.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No agility, no resilience: A wake-up call for Malaysia's manufacturers
Article:
MANUFACTURING sits at the heart of Malaysia's industrial ambitions and export competitiveness.
Today, it contributes roughly 24 per cent of the country's gross domestic product and accounts for around 80 per cent of total exports.
Yet, amid wild fluctuations in global trade, the nation's manufacturers face mounting pressure from macroeconomic headwinds, supply chain disruptions, rising input costs and increasingly complex operational demands.
These conditions are further complicated by production networks that span multiple jurisdictions, tightened sustainability requirements and heightened customer expectations on speed and availability.
The reality is that resilience must be baked into the operational core of the business. It is not just some nebulous contingency plan, but must be a real, strategic imperative to protect margins, sustain competitiveness and seize emerging opportunities.
Opportunity Meets Pressure
For Malaysia's manufacturing sector to experience continued success, much will depend on how effectively it adapts to a new set of structural realities.
On the one hand, the country has strong industrial fundamentals. It can boast established export capabilities, strategic geographic positioning and a diversified manufacturing base.
On the other hand, productivity gaps relative to more advanced economies, uneven levels of digital maturity and shortages of specialised technical talent remain persistent hurdles.
Simultaneously, a fealty to traditional production models means Malaysian manufacturers risk being left behind amid an increasingly volatile environment marked by rapid demand shifts, increased digital adoption, and supply chain disruptions.
The government's New Industrial Master Plan 2030 recognises these dynamics.
Its ambition to develop 3,000 smart factories and shift Malaysia towards high-value, technology-driven industries by 2030 signals the need to weather these factors through modernisation.
However, policy direction alone will not be enough to see this vision through to the finish line, with the onus equally on manufacturers to rethink operations.
That begins with a data architecture that connects the entire shop floor.
Unfortunately, many firms remain faithful to fragmented systems and siloed data despite the lack of real-time visibility and slowed down decision-making they engender.
Supply chain management, in particular, remains mired by a reactive mindset, which results in disruptions only being addressed after they occur.
For Malaysia's manufacturers, this is untenable. If it is not addressed now, local firms will find that they can no longer compete with the world's best.
Agility A Competitive Advantage
Traditional models prioritise scale and cost optimisation, but manufacturers must now think about flexibility, responsiveness, and coordinated execution across the value chain.
By shifting the focus to predictive planning, powered by integrated data and advanced analytics, manufacturers can better anticipate risks, optimise inventory, and maintain production continuity.
The advantages are clear. Greater operational visibility enables risks - such as supply shortages or sudden demand shifts - to be identified and resolved in near real time.
This safeguards production continuity and enhances cost control by reducing waste, optimising inventory, and improving procurement decisions.
Even during volatile times, firms will be better equipped to demonstrate reliability to customers through shorter lead times and greater product customisation offerings.
They will also be able to accelerate innovation via faster prototyping, iterative development, and closer collaboration across teams and partners.
That shortens go-to-market and empowers firms to capture opportunities in higher-value segments.
At the heart of this transformation are integrated digital platforms.
Enterprise resource planning systems connect data across production, procurement, inventory, and distribution to provide a unified,
real-time view of operations.
This enables faster, more informed decisions while helping manufacturers detect issues early, adjust production schedules swiftly and maintain tighter cost control.
Combined with analytics driven by artificial intelligence, these platforms unlock predictive capabilities that enable more accurate demand forecasts, pre-emptive fixes for equipment failures and proactive production planning.
Turning Resilience Into Long-Term Competitiveness
As global production networks become more interconnected, stronger regional supply chain integration will be essential.
Diversified and collaborative networks can reduce exposure to external shocks while enhancing continuity.
Digital collaboration will play a pivotal role in this shift. Shared platforms and data-driven coordination can improve transparency, streamline workflows, and enable more agile decision-making across suppliers, partners, and internal teams.
This deep integration strengthens the entire value chain, making it more responsive and robust.
Malaysia is well-positioned to lead in advanced manufacturing. However, translating its extant advantages into sustained competitiveness rests on how quickly and effectively firms embrace a connected shop floor.
The path forward requires deliberate and shrewd investments in digital capabilities, workforce development, and adaptive supply chain strategies.
This will define the sector's efforts to boost productivity and scale smart manufacturing.
Ultimately, resilience is about building the capacity to evolve, adapt and lead.
By embedding agility at the core of manufacturing operations, Malaysian manufacturers can strengthen their position in the global value chains and drive sustainable, long-term economic growth.
*The writer is Epicor vice president for Asia
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police probe Facebook user for insulting Sultan of Selangor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have launched an investigation into a Facebook account owner over a post deemed insulting to the Sultan of Selangor.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the April 25 post by the Facebook account 'Huayan The Pureland Learning Centre' has been traced, with action underway.
"Investigations against the individual are ongoing, including the seizure of devices used to upload the post by the CID's Classified Criminal Investigation Unit, Prosecution and Legal Division (D5), in collaboration with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).
"To date, police have received a total of 21 reports nationwide in relation to the same issue.
"A total of 21 witnesses, including those who lodged reports, have been called to have their statements recorded to assist in the investigation," he said in a statement.
Kumar said the case is being investigated under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, which involves acts with a seditious tendency, carrying a fine of up to RM5,000 or imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
"In addition, investigations are also being carried out under Section 504 of the Penal Code, for intentional insult with intent to provoke a breach of the peace, which carries imprisonment of up to two years or a fine, or both.
"We are also investigating under Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 for improper use of network facilities, which carries a fine of up to RM500,000 or imprisonment of up to two years, or both," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Politicising FWCMS may set back progress made in managing foreign workers: Mamba
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Politicising Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) could undermine the progress made in improving governance, efficiency and transparency in foreign worker recruitment, Malaysian Micro Business Association (Mamba) said.
Mamba president Tan Peng Beng said the implementation of FWCMS and wider digitalisation efforts had helped resolve longstanding weaknesses in the foreign worker recruitment system.
"The transition from fragmented, manual processes to a centralised digital platform has resolved many of the inefficiencies and leakages that once enabled abuse, delays and opacity," he said.
Tan said micro businesses and SMEs including hawkers, market traders, pasar malam operators, small sundry shop owners and workshops are the backbone of Malaysia's economy and depend heavily on migrant workers to sustain daily operations.
"Any disruption to the system will not just affect large corporations, but will disproportionately impact micro businesses and small enterprises, creating uncertainty, operational disruptions and financial hardship for those who can least afford it," he said.
He added that from the perspective of businesses on the ground, the system is functioning well and has delivered clear improvements.
"For many employers, particularly within the micro and SME segments, the system works.
"It has improved processing timelines, enhanced transparency and provided a more structured and reliable framework that businesses can depend on," he said.
Tan noted that FWCMS has operated successfully under five different government administrations, reflecting both the strength of the system and strict processes supporting its implementation and oversight.
"Very few systems can demonstrate continuity and relevance across multiple administrations. This alone speaks volumes about the system's resilience and institutional value," he said.
Many past inefficiencies especially on the demand side involving employer applications and approvals had been significantly reduced through digitalisation, he said.
However, he acknowledged that challenges remain, particularly those beyond Malaysia's jurisdiction.
This includes issues in source countries such as recruitment practices, documentation integrity and worker welfare.
"These are structural and cross-border issues that cannot be resolved by Malaysia alone.
"What is needed is the continued evolution of solutions, including the use of more advanced digital technologies, cross-border integration and stronger international cooperation," he said.
While public debate on FWCMS is necessary and healthy, Tan said discussions must remain constructive and fact-based rather than politically driven.
"We welcome scrutiny and discussion, but it must be responsible. Politicising the system without a full appreciation of its impact risks reversing the gains that have been made."
He urged policymakers, industry players, civil society and technology providers to work together towards a more holistic and sustainable solution.
"Our collective priority should be to protect the welfare of migrant workers, eliminate profiteering and ensure that Malaysian businesses, particularly micro businesses and SMEs, are supported by a fair, efficient and transparent system."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Pas signals early push to unseat Syed Saddiq in Muar
Article:
MUAR: Pas has signalled an early push to capture the Muar parliamentary seat from incumbent Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman.
Johor Pas commissioner Datuk Dr Mahfodz Mohamed confirmed the party is preparing for a tougher contest at the next general election.
Mahfodz said the party has begun strengthening its election machinery in the constituency and across the district, anticipating an intense multi-cornered fight.
"We won Maharani, so we must defend it. Our machinery is being strengthened early as we expect the political competition to be more intense," he said at the Johor Pas election machinery at the Muar Pas community centre in Parit Tarom here last night.
Muar has been a closely watched battleground since Syed Saddiq first captured the seat in 2018 under the Pakatan Harapan banner.
At 25, he became one of the country's youngest members of parliament.
He retained the seat in the 2022 general election under his own party, Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda), cementing his grip on the constituency despite shifting national alliances.
At the state level, however, Pas made inroads in Johor during the 2022 state polls by winning the Maharani seat, which falls within the Muar parliamentary constituency.
Mahfodz said Pas's hold on Maharani would serve as a springboard to strengthen its position across Muar.
He said the party will focus on consolidating grassroots support, enhancing voter outreach, and coordinating with Perikatan Nasional partners.
Mahfodz, who is not currently an MP or state assemblyman, previously served as the Bukit Pasir assemblyman in Muar for one term after winning the seat in 1999.
Political observers describe Muar as a hybrid seat where urban youth voters, traditional Malay heartland support, and shifting party loyalties intersect, making it one of the state's most unpredictable constituencies.
The declaration by Pas sets the stage for a high-stakes contest pitting its machinery against Syed Saddiq's personal brand and youth appeal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM26mil allocated for Menu Kasih Siswa programme
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry has introduced the Menu Kasih Siswa programme, with an allocation of RM26 million.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said university administrators should not wait for formal ministerial launches before rolling out the initiative.
It is aimed at providing affordable meals to students, particularly those from underprivileged backgrounds.
"We have actually already launched the Menu Kasih Siswa programme. I have personally visited several institutions, such as UniMAP and UniSZA, to oversee its roll-out.
"However, we have directed all universities that they must not wait for a minister to be present for an official ceremony before they start.
"This is about government policy and helping our students who are facing financial hardships. We must move quickly," Zambry said at the second Malaysia-Republic of Türkiye Joint Committee meeting today.
He said the ministry is also extending the programme to private higher education institutions through a two-pronged strategy.
"Regarding private higher education institutions, we are taking two approaches. Firstly, we have requested them to hold discussions with the Director-General to ensure that food prices in their cafeterias and canteens remain affordable.
"Secondly, we are engaging with the National Student Representative Council, which includes student leaders from private universities, to determine how we can best channel aid and assistance to the intended target groups," he added.
He said the government has increased the programme's budget to ensure its success.
"There was an initial additional budget of RM15 million. With subsequent additions from the government, we now have a total of RM26 million for this purpose," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan MB to meet Ruler as BN withdraws support
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun is believed to have sought an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today following a state leadership crisis.
The meeting follows a shock announcement by all 14 Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen that they have unanimously withdrawn their support for the Aminuddin-led state government.
Aminuddin, who attended an awards ceremony at SMK Datuk Muhammad Yusof earlier today, reportedly left the event in haste at 11am to meet the ruler, who is believed to be in Kuala Lumpur.
Earlier today, state BN chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias held a press conference to announce the withdrawal of support, citing a crisis involving state constitutional and customary institutions.
The political turmoil follows a controversial move by the Undang Luak (territorial chiefs), who reportedly declared the removal of Tuanku Muhriz at the Balai Undang Luak Sungai Ujong.
In response to the customary dispute, Aminuddin had previously stated that the Negri Sembilan Council of Justice and Law advised the Luak Sungai Ujong to accept the termination of Datuk Mubarak Dohak as the Datuk Klana Petra.
The unfolding crisis has already disrupted legislative business, with the state assembly sitting originally scheduled for four days starting last Friday, being postponed indefinitely despite being officially opened by Tuanku Muhriz on Thursday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PN offers to form new Negri Sembilan govt with BN
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Perikatan Nasional (PN) has offered to collaborate with Barisan Nasional (BN) to form a new state government, hours after all 14 BN assemblymen withdrew their support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
State opposition leader and Labu assemblyman Mohamad Hanifah Abu Baker announced the decision following an emergency meeting held here today.
Hanifah said the five PN assemblymen are ready to work with the 14 BN representatives to ensure administrative stability and the continued development of the state.
"Representing the people of Negri Sembilan, we regret that this crisis has been allowed to drag on, affecting the effectiveness of the state administration to the point that the state assembly sitting had to be postponed indefinitely.
"This proves that Aminuddin has failed to handle this crisis responsibly in the interest of the people. Accordingly, all five PN assemblymen are prepared to cooperate with the 14 Umno-BN assemblymen to guarantee the stability of the state government," he said.
Also present were Ridzuan Ahmad (Gemas), Abdul Fatah Zakaria (Bagan Pinang), Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa (Serting), and Kamarul Ridzuan Mohd Zain (Paroi).
Hanifah, who is also the state Bersatu chief, said the crisis reached a critical point after the BN bloc unanimously declared a loss of confidence in Aminuddin's leadership.
During the press conference, he handed a letter of cooperation to Negri Sembilan Umno secretary Datuk Mustapha Nagoor.
Mustapha told reporters that PN's proposal would be conveyed to the state Umno chairman, Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias.
Earlier, PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan was seen attending a meeting with the state coalition leadership at the hotel before the announcement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia shifts focus to tech fields in Türkiye education collaboration
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is shifting its higher education collaboration with Türkiye towards technology and technical fields, moving beyond traditional areas such as literature and history.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the move will be a key focus of the second Malaysia–Türkiye Joint Committee (JC) meeting, which Malaysia will host this year.
He said the collaboration builds on the agreement signed between both countries to strengthen cooperation in higher education through structured engagement and annual meetings.
"Previously, Malaysian students who pursued their studies in Türkiye were mostly in fields such as literature, history and Ottoman studies, including learning the Turkish language.
"However, we recognise that Turkish universities have strong capabilities in technology-related fields, and we are now expanding our focus in that direction," he said at a media briefing at Menara Dato' Onn today.
Zambry said Malaysia has identified suitable higher education institutions in Türkiye for collaboration, particularly in areas such as artificial intelligence (AI), aerospace, green technology, sustainability and digital transformation.
He added that efforts are underway to broaden student mobility into these fields, in line with national priorities and global demand.
"This includes plans to send more Malaysian students to Türkiye to pursue studies in technical and technology disciplines," he said.
Zambry said the initiative also involves Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara), following an announcement by the Deputy Prime Minister.
"Mara has agreed in principle to send its students in technical and technology fields to Türkiye, and this will also involve lecturers to gain greater exposure in these areas," he said.
The collaboration will involve public universities, government-linked universities, polytechnics and private higher education institutions from both countries.
Zambry said both nations are also expected to strengthen joint research, capacity building and academic exchanges, including training programmes for students, lecturers and researchers.
He described the joint committee as a key platform to deepen bilateral ties and ensure cooperation delivers meaningful outcomes.
"This collaboration between Malaysia and Türkiye, particularly in education and technology, will benefit both countries in the long term," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melor assemblyman to give statement over viral video mocking police
Article:
KOTA BARU: Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud will present himself at the Kota Baru district police headquarters this afternoon to provide a statement regarding allegations of disparaging the police.
Wan Rohimi is expected to hold a press conference following the process at 5pm.
Kelantan police previously confirmed an investigation into an individual for making statements belittling the security forces in a viral video related to a motorcycle convoy held two days ago.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the probe is being conducted under Section 504 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955.
Yusoff described the remarks as
irresponsible and provocative,
noting they could damage the image, reputation and credibility of the force.
On Saturday, the event organised by the Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) and Kelantan Pas Youth drew heavy criticism on social media.
The backlash followed the release of the programme itinerary, which listed Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) in Pengkalan Chepa as one of the transit locations.
The convoy, led by Wan Rohimi, began around 5pm and travelled from Kota Lama through Pengkalan Chepa towards the city centre.
However, the participants did not enter the LTSIP compound and only passed the building on the way to Pantai Sabak, as authorities were stationed at the airport entrance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU steelmakers set for rebound as Iran war hurts more exposed Asian peers
Article:
After over half a dozen muted earnings seasons, steelmakers in the European Union (EU) are set for a rebound and the first quarter of 2026 could be an inflection point, analysts say.
Though demand has not recovered to 2022 levels, steel prices have increased faster than expected in recent months thanks to higher energy prices and diminishing imports from outside the bloc due to the EU's new safeguards.
European hot rolled coil prices have gained about 20 per cent in the past six months.
Barring demand, the planets are now more or less aligned for the sector, Oddo BHF analyst Maxime Kogge told Reuters. "In the past, we only had some one-off measures against imports. Now the system has been fundamentally structurally strengthened," Kogge said, referring to the EU's carbon levy on high-emission imports and trade policy to halve import quotas, set to be in place from July 1.
Higher steel prices will help boost EU steelmakers, which are almost all expected to post higher first-quarter profits compared to the same period last year and to the previous quarter, according to data compiled by LSEG.
Surprisingly, the war in the Middle East has also benefited them in some ways, though probably not enough to outweigh the drawbacks. Despite the conflict increasing uncertainty, threatening to stall further investments and purchases, and causing a surprise contraction of business activity in the euro zone, it seems to have made European steelmakers more competitive.
"Especially in commodity and specialty chemicals, Asian peers are a lot more affected than their European counterparts because their dependence on energy from the Middle East is much higher," said Hansjoerg Pack, senior portfolio equity manager at German asset manager DWS.
Higher shipping costs have helped "regionalise" the steel market and buying behaviour has seen a change in Europe, analysts from Bank of America wrote to investors earlier this month, as customers have shifted purchases towards domestic producers due to fears of supply disruptions.
DEMAND DESTRUCTION
Still, questions linger. Following the flare-up in the Middle East, the World Steel Association in April trimmed its 2026 forecast for steel demand in the EU and Britain from 3.2 per cent to 1.3 per cent. That will weigh down the recovery.
Energy also remains a concern. European steelmakers face industrial power prices more than 50 per cent higher than their Chinese and Indian rivals and more than twice those of US producers, Bank of America warned in a separate note. Moreover, the industry was counting on the German defence plan, which "hasn't delivered anything so far", Oddo BHF's Kogge said, adding that prior hopes the plan would start impacting order intakes from the second half of the year would not materialise.
The pace of the recovery will now hinge on the developments of the war in the Middle East, the analysts said, as higher inflation could lead the European Central Bank to hike interest rates and stall demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Welfare Dept seeks families of two abandoned babies in Johor
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Welfare Department is seeking to trace the biological parents of a baby girl found in a rubbish disposal area at a flats complex in Taman Plentong Utama here on March 9.
District welfare officer Khairu Asri Ismail said, based on a police report, a resident at Block G heard a baby crying at 8.40am and traced the sound.
The infant, with her umbilical cord still attached, was found abandoned at the flats' rubbish disposal area.
"The resident then lodged a police report and the baby was rushed to Sultan Ismail Hospital," Khairu said in a statement.
Medical checks confirmed that the infant had been born just hours before she was dumped.
The hospital reported her to be in good health, with development consistent with a normal newborn.
The department classified the infant as a child in need of care and protection, believed to have been abandoned or neglected by her parents or guardians.
She was named Lea Athea and is currently placed at the Mini Children's Home in Ulu Tiram under an interim care order issued by the Children's Court here under Section 30(7) of the Child Act 2001.
The department has also obtained permission from the court to publish the child's photograph to assist in tracing her next of kin.
"Those with any information that may help identify the child's parents and ensure her welfare and future well-being are urged to contact Johor Baru Welfare Department officer Jamalluddin Hassan at 07-2282971 or 07-2282972.
The department is also seeking the family members of a two-month-old baby boy found abandoned at the staircase of Block A, ARC Residence, Jalan Sagu 36, Taman Daya on Feb 23.
The infant, named Farel Zayyan, is placed under temporary protection under a similar interim court order.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China vows countermeasures if EU enacts 'Made in Europe' plan
Article:
BEIJING: Beijing slammed on Monday an EU plan aimed to bolster the bloc's industries against fierce competition from China, vowing countermeasures if it is enacted.
The EU unveiled in March new "Made in Europe" rules for companies trying to access public funds in strategic sectors including cars, green tech and steel, obliging firms to meet minimum thresholds for EU-made parts.
The proposal, held up for months by wrangling over the measures, is a key part of a European Union drive to regain its competitive edge, reduce its industrial decline and stave off hundreds of thousands of job losses.
Beijing's commerce ministry said on Monday that it had submitted comments to the European Commission on Friday, expressing China's "serious concerns" regarding the act it called "systemic discrimination."
"If the EU... presses ahead with the legislation, and thereby harms the interests of Chinese companies, China will have no choice but to take countermeasures to firmly safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of its enterprises," the commerce ministry warned in a statement.
European businesses in many of the sectors concerned by the proposal have long lamented they face unfair competition from heavily subsidised Chinese rivals.
The EU proposal, formally known as the "Industrial Accelerator Act", implicitly targets Chinese makers of batteries and electric vehicles by requiring foreign firms to partner with European firms and pass on technological know-how when setting up shop in the bloc.
The Chinese Chamber of Commerce to the EU said this month the plan marked a shift towards protectionism that would affect trade cooperation between the EU and China.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil price hike does not guarantee higher Sabah revenue
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The rise in global oil prices does not necessarily translate into higher state revenue for oil-producing Sabah.
State Assistant Finance Minister Datuk Mohd Ishak Ayub said the conflict in the Middle East since early March 2026 has led to a significant increase in global oil prices.
Based on data from the US Energy Information Administration, the average price of Brent crude oil rose from USD71 per barrel in February 2026 to USD103.00 per barrel in March 2026, representing an increase of approximately 45.5 per cent.
"However, the increase in prices does not fully translate into higher state revenue.
"This is because the stronger ringgit against the US dollar has reduced the value of sales when converted into the local currency. The exchange rate at present, or at market close on April 24, is RM3.97," he said in reply to a question from Datuk Wong Hong Jun (Warisan–Tanjung Aru) during a question-and-answer session at the Sabah Legislative Assembly sitting here.
Ishak said the actual impact on State Sales Tax (SST) revenue will only be known once payments are received.
In accordance with Section 14 of the State Sales Tax Enactment 1998, SST payments for the taxable periods of March and April 2026 will be due on April 28 and May 28, respectively.
"To provide an initial indication, the ministry has prepared preliminary projections based on current performance assumptions, including sales volume, oil prices, prevailing exchange rates and the SST rate of 5 per cent.
"Based on these assumptions, total SST collection from Petronas and its subsidiaries for the period from March to April 2026 is projected to reach RM96.5 million, reflecting an estimated increase of RM8.9 million, or 23 per cent per month, compared with collections in February 2026.
"However, this figure is subject to change depending on market developments."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM30mil Media Innovation Fund to boost local media industry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry has allocated RM30 million under the Media Innovation Fund to strengthen the local media industry through grants of up to RM300,000 per application.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the funding covers four key areas, namely training for media personnel, software and technology adaptation, equipment and infrastructure support, as well as content research and development.
She said Phase Five of the Media Innovation Fund will open from Friday (May 1) until May 14.
"The Media Innovation Fund has progressed through four phases to date, with Phase One completed in January 2026, involving 34 organisations receiving RM12.38 million for digital transformation.
"Phases Two to Four were implemented through successive application windows opened in February, March and April, and are currently at the approval committee stage.
"Phase Five of the Media Innovation Fund will open from Friday (May 1) until May 14, and we encourage media organisations that have yet to apply to take advantage of the opportunity," she said in her speech at the opening of the 21st Confederation of Asean Journalists here today.
Teo said the initiative is part of efforts to revitalise traditional media and ensure the industry remains relevant amid rapid technological changes, particularly the rise of artificial intelligence (AI).
She added that the government is also supporting the industry through AI-focused training programmes under the Tun Abdul Razak Broadcasting and Information Institute (IPPTAR).
Since its introduction in 2024, 55 AI-related courses have been planned, of which 41 have been conducted as of March 31 this year, involving 1,552 participants.
For 2026, 20 AI courses have been planned, with six already implemented, involving 194 participants.
She said these efforts are aimed at equipping media practitioners with the necessary skills to use AI technologies effectively, while ensuring Malaysia's media industry remains resilient and competitive in the digital era.
Earlier, Teo said artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly reshaping how news is produced and consumed, but journalists must continue to safeguard credibility and public trust.
She said the media landscape is undergoing major transformation as AI tools become increasingly integrated into newsroom operations, enabling faster processing of information, trend detection and multilingual reporting.
"AI will play a bigger role in the future of journalism, but it must remain a supporting tool, not a replacement for human judgment and accountability," she said.
Teo highlighted that reliance on AI could also reinforce dominant global narratives if not carefully managed, especially as much international news content is still influenced by Western media organisations.
National Union of Journalists Malaysia president Low Boon Tat, who was appointed as the new Confederation of Asean Journalists president, said while AI improves efficiency, journalism still depends on human experience and on-the-ground reporting.
"Journalism is not just about information. It is about understanding, experience and being physically present to verify what is happening," he said.
He said journalists remain essential to ensure stories reflect local perspectives rather than automated repetition of global viewpoints.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Scam content takedowns hit 43,618 in Q1, Teo Nie Ching calls trend worrying
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — A total of 43,618 scam-related content was removed by social media platforms as of the first quarter of this year, said Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching.
She said the removals were carried out at the request of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), with platform providers complying with the applications.
Teo said the number of scam-related content taken down has risen sharply each year, with 6,297 cases recorded in 2023, increasing to 63,652 in 2024 and 98,503 in 2025.
“This trend is worrying. It is crucial for all parties, including the media, to work together to educate the public on digital literacy,” she said after officiating the 21st General Assembly of the Asean Journalists Federation here today.
She added that not all requests submitted by MCMC were acted upon by platform providers. Since 2023, a total of 255,931 takedown requests have been issued, of which 212,070 have been complied with.
Meanwhile, in her speech when officiating the programme, Teo emphasised that the threats of scams, fake news and deepfakes are no longer local concerns, but a growing global challenge affecting countries across regions.
She reaffirmed Malaysia’s commitment to strengthening regional cooperation in combating misinformation, including establishing clearer standards on the use of artificial intelligence in media content. — Bernama

Title: DOE warns of jail, million‑ringgit fines as probe launched into Terengganu peatland fire
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The Department of Environment (DOE) has opened an investigation into parties suspected of carrying out open burning in connection with a forest fire in a peatland area at Kampung Baru, Kuala Abang in Dungun, Terengganu, on April 22.
The DOE views the forest fire incident in the peatland area seriously and that an investigation has been initiated under Section 29A of the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
“The department would like to stress that if the investigation finds any offences that violate the law, firm action will be taken under Section 29A of the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
“If found guilty, offenders may be fined not less than RM25,000 and not exceeding RM1 million, or imprisoned for a term not exceeding five years, or both,” the department said in a statement.
It added that it had conducted an investigation at the incident site after receiving information about the fire and found that the blaze involved a government reserve land area of approximately 32 hectares.
“The fire has produced thick smoke that could potentially affect air quality and impact nearby residents and sensitive areas, including several educational institutions,” it said.
Hence, the department, in line with its jurisdiction under the Environmental Quality Act 1974, has also taken several measures, including continuous air quality monitoring through the installation of portable monitoring equipment (GRIMM) in areas near the fire location.
“To date, Terengganu DOE has installed one unit of air quality monitoring equipment at Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Baru Kuala Abang, Dungun, and an additional monitoring unit in the affected area.
The department has also provided Air Pollutant Index (API) readings to relevant parties, including the district office and the district education office. The API reading in the affected area as of 8 am today was 88, which is at a moderate level.
“The DOE is also monitoring the fire from the air using drones to assess the extent of the affected area, and is working closely with the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) and other agencies in efforts to control and put out the fire.
The department also advised the public to avoid any open burning activities, especially during hot and dry weather conditions that could accelerate the spread of fires.
“The DOE will continue to closely monitor the situation and ensure that pollution control measures are implemented to protect public health and the environment,” it said. — Bernama

Title: Pilgrims warned: ‘No Visa, No Haj’ as Putrajaya enforces strict compliance with Saudi rules
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — The
Tiada Visa, Tiada Haji
(No Visa, No Haj) compliance campaign has been launched to safeguard the welfare, safety and well being of pilgrims, curb fraud and ensure compliance with the regulations set by the Saudi government.
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Datuk Dr Zulkifli Hasan, said the campaign is being implemented in response to the Saudi government’s call to promote a similar approach.
“Through this campaign, we are working for the benefit of all pilgrims and the safety of the entire Muslim community, aiming to prevent fraud and scams that harm Muslims in Malaysia.
“So, this campaign is about ensuring that all our haj arrangements and management are well organised, orderly and truly able to meet everything our pilgrims expect,” he told reporters after attending the monthly agency gathering at the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) here today.
Zulkifli said the campaign also aligns with the Muzakarah Haji’s ruling on the importance of complying with haj visa regulations.
He said the 1447H/2026M haj season has so far run smoothly, with orderly management and good flight operations, adding that Tabung Haji (TH) continues to prioritise public safety as one of the campaign’s main missions.
“Only pilgrims with valid visas will be allowed to perform haj, which helps protect their welfare throughout their stay in the Holy Land,” he said.
He also reminded Malaysians not to perform haj independently, as they would not be covered for any problems, such as health issues, if they did not go through official channels.
He stressed that using any visa other than a haj visa, such as tourist, business, work or umrah visas, for the purpose of performing haj is an offence under Saudi law.
“Entry into Makkah for haj is only allowed for those with a valid haj visa or official documents such as an Iqamah or work permit in the Holy Land area,” he said.
He said anyone who breaks the rules can face fines of up to SR100,000 (RM105,377), while those who help or transport pilgrims without valid visas could be jailed for up to six months and fined up to SR50,000 (RM52,698). — Bernama

Title: Police probe fatal Klang crash after viral video shows bystanders breaking mirror, moving vehicle
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 27 — Police are investigating a fatal accident in Pandamaran, Klang, after a viral video showed several individuals breaking a vehicle’s mirror and attempting to move it following the crash.
In a statement, South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident occurred on April 26 at about 8.30pm along Jalan Bukit Kerayong.
Preliminary investigations found the crash involved a Toyota Avanza driven by a local man in his 60s, who was travelling from Jalan Bukit Kerayong towards Jalan Banting Pandamaran.
Police believe the driver lost control of the vehicle and crashed into a stone structure near a house by the roadside, causing the vehicle to end up at the edge of a drain.
Lim said actions by members of the public who attempted to move the vehicle resulted in it falling into the drain with the victim inside.
The victim was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, in conjunction with a sudden death report.
Police are still working to determine the exact cause of the incident.
Members of the public with information or who witnessed the incident are urged to come forward to assist investigations by contacting investigating officer Insp Ahmad Syahid Salahudin at 011-35753557 or visiting the nearest police station.
Lim also advised road users to remain alert, stay focused while driving and adhere to traffic rules to prevent similar tragedies.

Title: Hari Raya travel surge lifts KLIA traffic, MAHB reports 14.4pc rise in Q1 passenger movements
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, April 27 — Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) recorded strong growth across its local network in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, with Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) registering 16.9 million passenger movements, a 14.4 per cent increase compared to the same period last year.
In a statement today, MAHB said that in March alone, local airports handled 9.1 million passenger movements, of which 5.6 million travelled through KLIA, while the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period from March 14 to 29 saw more than 3.1 million passengers passing through the airport.
MAHB managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said the continued growth across the network demonstrates the increasing relevance of Malaysia’s airports as regional gateways.
“Our priority is to match this momentum with the right capacity, seamless operations and targeted improvements on the ground, ensuring we remain competitive while unlocking further growth opportunities for Malaysia,” he said.
MAHB said during the same month, Penang International Airport (PEN) saw an 11 per cent rise in international passenger numbers, moving from 371,601 in February to 412,958. Meanwhile, Kota Kinabalu International Airport (BKI) recorded a two per cent increase in domestic passenger numbers, rising from 541,248 in February to 551,049.
It said this contributed to a total of 27.4 million passenger movements across the local network in the first quarter.
“MAHB’s wholly owned international subsidiary, Istanbul Sabiha Gokcen International Airport (ISGIA), recorded 11.7 million passengers in the first quarter of 2026, representing a 12 per cent increase compared to the corresponding period last year,” it added.
Meanwhile, the airport operator said ongoing improvements to airport facilities and services are being implemented to enhance passenger comfort and operational efficiency across its network.
At KLIA, it said a large-scale ketupat-inspired installation brought festive cheer to the terminal, seamlessly blending cultural identity with modern design. Regional airports are also seeing targeted improvements, with Langkawi (LGK) and Kuching (KCH) introducing upgraded baby care rooms, while Alor Star (AOR), Kuantan (KUA), Labuan (LBU) and Lahad Datu (LDU) now welcome passengers with refreshed check-in counters.
